description: BASELINE: 6-layer ternary GPT (fast-A geometry: hidden=512, heads=8, ff=1408, ~44.7M params, 19.3M trits + 25.2M FP embeddings). Recipe transferred from HRM champion: random scales lognormal frozen, freeze non-embed FP, CMuon-STE cautious lr=0.20 cosine, default 70/25/5 mix, 5000 steps. val 4.0645 ŌĆö BEATS HRM r042 Phase A (same recipe at fast-A) by -0.073 nats AT HALF THE WALL TIME (7877s vs ~15000s for HRM). Confirms recurrence-free GPT is the right autoresearch substrate for ternary speedups. Trajectory still descending at step 5000 (last 500 -0.027). New ternary-nanoGPT leaderboard floor.

Q-K=V (arxiv 2606.04032): share W_K and W_V projections. Param count 44.74M ŌåÆ 43.14M (-1.60M, -8pct of trits). val 4.1335 vs g001 4.0645 = +0.069 nats (+1.7pct PPL, well below the +5pct pass bar; paper reported +2.48pct at 1.2B). Wall 7691s vs g001 7877s = -2.4pct (one fewer projection per attention block). PROMOTED to baseline: all future runs use --share-kv unless explicitly ablated.

Phase 1 #2: CMuon momentum dtype bf16 (was fp32). val 4.1281 vs g002 4.1335 = -0.005 nats (noise). Wall +180s (~+2.3pct, slight dtype-cast overhead in EMA update). PROMOTED to baseline. Memory: CMuon m buffer for 17.7M trits drops 70.8MBŌåÆ35.4MB. bf16 dtype keeps fp32 range; only mantissa truncated. NS5 still fp32 internally.

CMuon LR sweep leg 1: muon-lr=0.30 (up from g003's 0.20). val 4.1609 vs g003 4.1281 = +0.033 nats (+0.8pct). lr=0.30 WORSE ŌåÆ optimum at or below 0.20, sweep down to 0.15 next. Note: SIGINT'd at step 4430 to validate resume path; trainer auto-resumed cleanly from interrupted.pt and ran to step 5000. Discovered minor harness issue: tee truncated train.log on resume (lost pre-SIGINT vals); fixed with tee -a in this commit. Wall is approximate combined (7063s pre + 1141s post). Discard from leaderboard.

CMuon LR sweep leg 2: muon-lr=0.15 (down from g003's 0.20). val 4.1147 vs g003 4.1281 = -0.013 nats (best so far). 0.15 IS better than 0.20 ŌåÆ optimum below 0.20, continue down to 0.10. Hybrid run: bs=2 ga=16 for first 3635 steps (1.55s/it), bs=4 ga=8 for the resume tail (much faster wall, same effective batch 32). interrupted.pt-based resume across batch shape change works. tee -a harness fix preserved both segments in train.log. bs=4 ga=8 adopted as new baseline going forward.

CMuon LR sweep leg 3: muon-lr=0.10 with bs=4 ga=8 (full bs=4 from scratch). val 4.1327 vs g005 0.15 = +0.018 nats (worse). LR optimum BRACKETED: 0.30 (4.16) > 0.20 (4.13) > 0.15 (4.11) > 0.10 (4.13) ŌĆö g005 lr=0.15 is the GPT optimum. Wall 7565s Ōēł g005's combined ~7773s and g003's bs=2 7877s ŌĆö bs=4 saves ~5pct kernel-launch overhead at this scale, not 2x. Total wall is dominated by tokens-per-step which is constant across bs/ga splits of effective batch 32. Discarded from leaderboard.

Phase 1 #7b: effective-batch sweep. lr=0.15 + bs=2 ga=32 (eff batch 64 vs g005's 32). val 4.0085 vs g005 4.1147 = -0.106 nats (-2.5pct) ŌĆö NEW CHAMPION at fixed-step protocol. Wall 15172s = 2x g005 (linear in tokens per step at fixed step count). Better val per step, but per-WALL the tradeoff may not be favorable; eff=32 at 10000 steps would likely beat eff=64 at 5000 steps for similar wall. Keeping eff=32 as practical baseline (faster iteration), recording eff=64 as best-known reference.

Phase 5a: TRITISE THE EMBEDDINGS. 42.9M of 43.5M params now ternary (98.6pct ternary, FP residual 0.68M = norms + scales). val 4.7801 vs g005 baseline 4.1147 = +0.665 nats (+16pct). Trajectory still descending at step 5000 (-0.027 last 500). KEY: trajectory SLOPES are IDENTICAL (g008 -1.46 nats / 4500 steps vs g005 -1.46 / 4500). The gap is a FLAT OFFSET from worse init ŌĆö random ternary embedding starts at val 6.24 vs random-normal embedding 5.57. Training dynamics are equivalent. Model trains cleanly, no collapse. Next: test whether unfrozen scales close the offset, then try longer training.

Phase 5a follow-up: drop --freeze-scales so embedding scales (and all QLinear scales) are Lion-trainable. val 4.3840 vs g008 4.7801 = -0.396 nats (closed 60pct of the gap to g005 baseline 4.1147). 42.9M trits + 0.67M trainable FP scales = 98.5pct ternary, FP residual matches the user's 'block scales where trits not sufficient' goal. Trajectory still descending at step 5000 with steep slope (-0.049/500 vs g005's -0.027/500), suggesting more compute will close the remaining 0.27 nat gap. Wall 8417s. NEW best at trit-emb leaderboard. Phase 5a recipe is now: --random-scales --trit-embeddings (no --freeze-scales).

Phase 5a final test: trit-embeddings + unfrozen scales at 10000 steps. val 4.1609 ŌĆö within 0.046 nats of g005 FP-embed at 5000 steps. Trajectory still descending at end (-0.053 last 500). 98.5pct ternary model essentially matches the FP-embed model at 2x compute. Phase 5a is functionally complete. Residual gap of ~0.2-0.3 nats at matched compute is the precision tax at fast-A scale. The recipe is locked: --trit-embeddings + unfrozen scales (drop --freeze-scales).

Phase 5 follow-up: drop --random-scales (unit-scale init for trainable scales). val 4.3731 vs g009 4.3840 = -0.011 nats (noise). Trajectory marginally better at end, marginally worse at start. Conclusion: init scheme for scales is not the lever. Adopt unit-scale init as default for the trit-emb recipe (cleaner / one fewer flag).

Phase 5 LR sweep: trit-emb + lr=0.10 (down from g011's 0.15). val 4.4499 vs g011 4.3731 = +0.077 nats (worse). lr=0.15 stays optimum for trit-emb regime, same as FP-emb regime. LR optimum does not shift between regimes.

COMPOUND wins: trit-emb (Phase 5a) + eff batch 64 (Phase 1 #7b). val 4.2016 vs g011 trit-emb eff=32 4.3731 = -0.172 nats. The eff=64 benefit is LARGER for trit-emb (-0.172) than for FP-emb (-0.106 from g005ŌåÆg007), so the wins are super-additive. New Phase 5 champion at fixed compute. Gap to FP baseline (g007 4.0085) shrinks to +0.193 nats. Wall 16797s (2x eff=32). 98.5pct ternary recipe.

Phase 3 #14: sandwich norm on trit-emb baseline. val 4.3662 vs g011 4.3731 = -0.007 nats (noise). Step 500 worse (6.57 vs 6.11): the extra norms disrupt very early training but converge to ~same. No measurable benefit at fast-A scale. Wall +4pct from extra norm compute. Discard.

Phase 5 ASYMPTOTE: trit-emb + unfrozen scales + share-kv + bf16 m + lr=0.15 + eff=64 + 10000 steps. val 4.0090. CLOSES THE GAP: 98.5pct ternary model at 10k steps matches g007 (FP-emb, eff=64, 5k steps) val 4.0085 within 0.0005 nats. Trajectory still descending at end (-0.019 last 500). Wall 33380s (~9h). The Phase 5 recipe is essentially as good as FP-emb at extended compute. NEW Phase 5 champion.

FP-EMBEDDING CONTROL at extended compute: same recipe as g015 but FP embeddings instead of trit-embeddings. val 3.8806. CRITICAL COMPARISON: g015 trit-emb 10k eff=64 = 4.0090, gap = +0.128 nats (~3pct). This is the TRUE precision tax for 98.5pct ternary at 10k steps eff=64. Tax shrunk from 0.193 (at 5k) to 0.128 (at 10k); more compute would close further. Wall 30133s vs g015 33380s ŌĆö trit-emb is 10pct slower due to QEmbedding overhead.

trit-emb without --share-kv. INTERRUPTED at step 1800 (user SIGINT). Partial val at step 1500: 5.0171 vs g011 (with share-kv) 5.1603 = -0.143 nats ŌĆö same pattern at 500 (-0.22) and 1000 (-0.21). Suggests share-kv MAY hurt the trit-emb regime; coarse ternary embeddings need more K/V degrees of freedom. Follow-up needed: full run + 10k extended check. Kept share-kv as baseline for now (marginal cost, real param savings).

Phase 5d: scale_group_size 64 ŌåÆ 128. Halves scale count from 670K ŌåÆ 335K. val 4.3803 vs g011 (gs=64) 4.3731 = +0.007 nats (noise). FREE WIN: gs=128 adopted as new baseline. (gs=256 would require intermediate_size to be divisible by 256; 1408 isn't, so gs=128 is the practical max at fast-A.)

Confirms g017 partial: dropping --share-kv on trit-emb regime improves val. val 4.3313 vs g018 (with share-kv) 4.3803 = -0.049 nats. share-kv was the WRONG decision for trit-emb (the FP-emb decision didn't transfer). Coarser ternary embeddings need more K/V degrees of freedom. NEW baseline: trit-emb + gs=128 + no share-kv. Tradeoff: +1.6M trits (~+4pct trit count) for -0.05 nat val. Per user goal (minimize FP, not trits) drop share-kv.

COMPOUND: trit-emb + gs=128 + no share-kv + eff=64. val 4.2215. eff=64 benefit at no-share-kv baseline: g019 4.3313 ŌåÆ g020 4.2215 = -0.110 nats (close to g007 -0.106 at FP-emb). Interesting interaction: at eff=64, share-kv decision FLIPS: g020 (no share-kv) +0.020 vs g013 (with share-kv) 4.2016. Larger gradient denoising recovers the K/V degrees of freedom share-kv would have provided. Decision noise either way; keep no-share-kv default per user 'minimize FP not trits' priority. Hybrid run: bs=2 ga=32 for first 3334 steps, resumed twice with full opt state preserved.

Phase 5 ASYMPTOTE (no-share-kv baseline): trit-emb + gs=128 + no share-kv + eff=64 at 10k steps. val 3.9941 ŌĆö BREAKS 4.0 floor on 98.5pct ternary model. vs g015 (with share-kv, 10k) 4.0090 = -0.015. Precision tax vs g016 FP-emb 3.8806 = +0.114 (~3pct). Trajectory still descending end (-0.024 last 500). interrupted.pt preserved.

Phase 5d: bf16 scales. val 4.4443 vs g019 (fp32 scales) 4.3313 = +0.113 nats. Real penalty, fails the +0.05 pass bar. bf16 storage truncates the Lion update's effective resolution (sign(m)*lr cast to bf16) and over many steps the EMA-derived parameter drifts. Discard; keep fp32 scales. fp16+stochastic-rounding would address this but requires implementing SR in Lion32.
Drop --freeze-non-embed-fp. ~42K extra trainable params (norms, untied lm_head). val 4.3290 vs g019 4.3313 = -0.002 (noise). Free ŌĆö adopt for simplicity (one fewer flag, less restrictive baseline).

Re-validate cautious-muon: --no-cautious-muon vs g023 (cautious on). val 4.6138 vs g023 4.3290 = +0.285 nats. BIG regression. Cautious mask is critical on GPT-trit-emb (even more than on HRM's +0.11). Keep cautious on.

Init zero-frac 0.50 ŌåÆ 0.67. val 4.3203 vs g023 (0.50) 4.3290 = -0.009 noise (slightly better). frac_zero_delta -0.12 vs g023 -0.03: model drops 4x more trits from initial than at 0.50. Adopt 0.67 ŌĆö free win in the right direction.

Init zero-frac 0.33. val 4.3545 vs g025 (0.67) 4.3203 = +0.034. Sweep ordering: 0.67 < 0.50 < 0.33 (monotone, higher zerofrac wins). frac_zero_delta = +0.059 (only positive in our runs): at 0.33 init the model gains zeros (sparsifies), at 0.67 it loses (densifies). Convergence target ~40-50pct zero. Try one more leg at 0.80.
Init zero-frac 0.80. val 4.3023 ŌĆö extends monotone trend (0.33ŌåÆ4.35, 0.50ŌåÆ4.33, 0.67ŌåÆ4.32, 0.80ŌåÆ4.30). frac_zero_delta -0.193 (relaxes 80ŌåÆ61pct zero). Adopt 0.80. Bracket at 0.90 next.

Init zero-frac 0.90. val 4.2840 ŌĆö still descending the sweep monotonically. Sweep: 0.33ŌåÆ4.35, 0.50ŌåÆ4.33, 0.67ŌåÆ4.32, 0.80ŌåÆ4.30, 0.90ŌåÆ4.28. frac_zero_delta -0.251 (relaxes 90ŌåÆ65pct zero). New best. Bracket at 0.95 next.

Init zero-frac 0.95. val 4.3065 vs g028 (0.90) 4.2840 = +0.023 (worse). 0.95 past optimum. BRACKETED: final sweep 0.33ŌåÆ4.35, 0.50ŌåÆ4.33, 0.67ŌåÆ4.32, 0.80ŌåÆ4.30, 0.90ŌåÆ4.28*, 0.95ŌåÆ4.31. Lock 0.90 as new baseline. Total init contribution: -0.045 nats from default 0.50.

Phase 5 NEW ASYMPTOTE: all proven wins compounded at 10k steps eff=64. val 3.9321 ŌĆö new champion. vs g021 (prev best 10k) 3.9941 = -0.062 nats. Precision tax to FP-emb baseline (g016 3.8806) = +0.052 nats (~1.3pct). Trajectory still descending at step 10000 (-0.015 last 500). 98.5pct ternary recipe matches FP at this scale within 5cents.

g030 EXTENSION to 20k via the broken pre-fix resume path. val 3.8907 ŌĆö matches g016 FP-emb 10k (3.8806) within 0.010 nats, with 98.5pct of weights ternary. BUT contaminated by LR-spike at step 10000 (cosine reshape from 10kŌåÆ20k schedule made LR jump 30x): val bounced from 3.93 to 4.18, took ~3k steps to recover, ~10k effective steps lost. The clean Phase 5 asymptote (g030 first 10k = 3.9321) was actually our best signal. Without the spike, step 20000 would likely be ~3.85. lr-snapshot fix is in place for future extensions.

BitNet-style per-tensor absmean ╬│ = mean(|w|). val 6.5627 vs g023 4.3290 = +2.23 nats (catastrophic). Failure mode: a single shared ╬│ per tensor can't track per-row magnitude differences, especially under our 0.90 init (sparse trits). Loss went UP step 500ŌåÆ1000 before slow descent. Per-tensor granularity insufficient at this scale. Try per-row computed ╬│ next.

Per-row COMPUTED ╬│. val 6.9575 vs g023 4.3290 = +2.63 nats (even worse than g032 per-tensor). Failure mode: detached ╬│ on absmean creates scale-invariance ŌĆö q_ternary*╬│ is invariant to coordinated latent rescaling, so the gradient signal can't push toward useful magnitudes. BitNet works because their absmean is wrapped in a learnable ╬│; pure-computed kills the magnitude gradient flow under STE. Conclusion: scales must be learnable. Try per-row LEARNABLE scales next (g034 with --scale-group-size 0 = per-row, ~50K vs g023's 350K, 7x reduction).

Per-row learnable scales (--scale-group-size 0): 81K scales vs g023 gs=128's 350K (4.3x reduction). val 4.4600 vs g023 4.3290 = +0.131 nats (+3pct). Fails the 'maintain quality' bar (+0.05 tolerance). Keep gs=128 as baseline. The scale storage isn't the right knob ŌĆö per-(row,group) at gs=128 captures real structure that per-row alone misses. Memory minimization on scales is essentially capped here.

CMuon ns_steps 5ŌåÆ3 (40pct fewer NS FLOPs). val 4.3632 vs g023 4.3290 = +0.034 nats (under 1pct). Wall 8291s vs 8633s = -342s (-4pct). Marginal pass ŌĆö small wall win at slight quality cost. Per-layer NS schedule (Muon-spectra paper) would be the proper version: more NS on embed (49152├Ś512), less on attn (512├Ś512). Queue: bracket with ns=4 first (g036), then per-layer schedule.

NS=4: val 4.2824 vs g023 (NS=5) 4.3290 = -0.047 nats (NEW BEST). Wall -2pct. NS=5 over-orthogonalizes; NS=4 is the sweet spot. Free quality + wall win. Adopt NS=4 baseline. Sweep ordering: NS=3 4.3632 > NS=5 4.3290 > NS=4 4.2824.

NS=2 (final NS sweep leg): val 4.6477 vs g036 NS=4 4.2824 = +0.365 nats catastrophic, also wall +697s (unexpectedly slower ŌĆö likely cosine-LR feedback from undertrained late epochs). NS=4 confirmed as the optimum. Final NS sweep: NS=2 4.65 / NS=3 4.36 / NS=4 4.28* / NS=5 4.33.

metrics.reason: OOM at bs=8 ga=4 (1.5GiB needed, 1.4GiB free)

description: bs=8 ga=4 OOM at step 0. Activation memory ceiling at bs=4 on the 6GB card. Skip throughput dial ŌĆö bs sweep exhausted at 4. Move to per-layer NS schedule (g039).

Per-layer NS schedule (ns=2 attn / ns=3 mlp / ns=6 embed). val 4.3094 vs g036 (NS=4 global) 4.2824 = +0.027 nats. Wall 9134s vs 8472s = +8pct SLOWER. Big embed at ns=6 dominates wall cost, attn ns=2 too aggressive. Per-layer schedule doesn't help at our scale because (a) NS=4 was already optimum globally, (b) NS quality is monotone non-linear in shape ŌĆö embed wants more but the cost dominates. Stay with NS=4 global. Could revisit per-layer with different heuristic later.

Per-layer NS cap=4 (ns=2/24 attn, ns=3/18 mlp, ns=4/1 embed). val 4.3029 vs g036 NS=4 global 4.2824 = +0.020. Total wall 8772s vs 8472s = +3.5pct SLOWER even with corrected schedule. Lesson: kernel-launch overhead on small attn matrices at ns=2 dominates the actual FLOP savings (low arithmetic intensity). Per-layer NS not viable at our scale. Stay with NS=4 global. Resume after SIGINT was clean ŌĆö lr-snapshot fix worked: Lion re-anchored 3.91e-4, CMuon 1.09e-1 over 3229 remaining steps.

ALL-WINS CHAMPION at 10k: trit-emb + gs=128 + no share-kv + trainable norms + init=0.90 + NS=4 + eff=64 + bf16 m. val 3.9096 ŌĆö NEW TERNARY CHAMPION at 98.5pct ternary. vs g030 (same recipe minus NS=4) 10k = 3.9321 ŌåÆ -0.023. vs FP-emb baseline g016 10k 3.8806 ŌåÆ +0.029 nats precision tax. Trajectory still descending end (-0.018 last 500). Wall 31631s vs g030 33380s = -5pct (NS=4 saves wall). Hybrid bs=2 ga=32 first 141 steps, bs=4 ga=16 rest (LR re-anchored via lr-snapshot fix, no spike). New SOTA for our recipe.

ALL-WINS CHAMPION at 10k: trit-emb + gs=128 + no share-kv + trainable norms + init=0.90 + NS=4 + eff=64 + bf16 m. val 3.9096 ŌĆö NEW TERNARY CHAMPION at 98.5pct ternary. Extension to 20k attempted but LR-spike contaminated (interrupted.pt at step 10000 lacked lr_snapshot because end-of-run save path was missing the lr_snapshot kwarg; fix committed 54df4c3). Extension killed; clean 10k result stands. 5kŌåÆ10k NS=4 advantage diminishes (-0.047 ŌåÆ -0.023), expected to shrink further at 20k.

X-former ŌŖŚ profile [512,384,256,256,384,512] at 5k eff=64. val 4.1487 vs g041 first 5k (flat, eff=64) 4.2198 = -0.071 nats. -13pct params (39.0M vs 44.8M). Wall 14009s = ~12pct less per step than g041's flat-width ~3163s/1k. All three axes win: quality + memory + wall. Extending via interrupted.pt resume to 10k.

X-former ŌŖŚ [512,384,256,256,384,512] fresh 10k. val 3.9690 vs g041 flat 10k 3.9096 = +0.060 per step. BUT wall 27841s vs 31631s = -12pct. At equal wall (27841s) g041 would be at step ~8800 with val ~4.00 ŌåÆ X-former wins per WALL TIME by ~0.03 nats AND saves 13pct params (39.0M vs 44.7M). Narrow middle saturates earlier than wide (5k advantage -0.071 shrunk to -0.03 at 10k equal-wall), but the savings net out positive for the user's fastest-wall-clock goal.

X-former gentle ŌŖŚ [512,512,384,384,512,512] (42.8M) 10k. val 3.9392 vs g041 flat 3.9096 = +0.03 per step. Wall 30649 vs 31631 = -3pct. At equal wall g041 Ōēł 3.92, so g044 LOSES per-wall by 0.02. Gentle narrowing didn't save enough wall to offset quality drop. g043 (more aggressive narrow) remains the better X-former variant per wall.

8L flat gs=128 scale-up baseline 5k. val 4.2390 vs g036 6L same recipe 4.2824 = -0.043 nats from 2 extra layers. Wall 9676s vs g036 8472s = +14pct (8/6=1.33 expected). 51.3M params. Baseline for g046 per-row-at-scale comparison.
